branch_to_clone_defaults_to_the_default_: Branch to clone (defaults to the default branch)
clone_a_git_repository_into_a_daytona_sa: Clone a Git repository into a Daytona sandbox and run install, build, or test commands. Use for CI-style checks, repo health monitoring, or validating changes.
code_to_run_in_the_sandbox: Code to run in the sandbox
combined_stdout_stderr_output_execute_co: Combined stdout/stderr output (execute command and run code operations)
cursor_for_the_next_page_list_sandboxes_: Cursor for the next page (list sandboxes operation)
describe_the_code_you_want_to_run: Describe the code you want to run
directory_to_list_defaults_to_working_di: Directory to list (defaults to working directory)
disk_space_to_allocate_in_gb: Disk space to allocate in GB
exit_code_execute_command_and_run_code_o: Exit code (execute command and run code operations)
file_name_upload_file_and_download_file_: File name (upload file and download file operations)
file_size_in_bytes_upload_file_and_downl: File size in bytes (upload file and download file operations)
file_to_upload_to_the_sandbox: File to upload to the sandbox
files_at_the_given_path_list_files_opera: Files at the given path (list files operation)
get_start_stop_and_delete_also_accept_th: Get, start, stop, and delete also accept the sandbox name; all other operations require the ID
integrate_daytona_into_your_workflow_to_: Integrate Daytona into your workflow to run AI-generated code in secure, isolated sandboxes. Create and manage sandboxes, execute shell commands, run Python, JavaScript, or TypeScript code, transfer files, and clone Git repositories.
language_of_the_code_to_run: Language of the code to run
list_stop_and_delete_daytona_sandboxes_t: List, stop, and delete Daytona sandboxes to control cost and tidy up environments. Use for scheduled cleanup or auditing what is currently running.
maximum_number_of_sandboxes_to_return: Maximum number of sandboxes to return
pagination_cursor_from_a_previous_respon: Pagination cursor from a previous response
password_or_access_token_for_private_rep: Password or access token for private repositories
password_or_token_for_private_repositori: Password or token for private repositories
path_in_the_sandbox_to_clone_into: Path in the sandbox to clone into
path_in_the_sandbox_trailing_slash_uploa: Path in the sandbox (trailing slash uploads into directory)
path_of_the_file_in_the_sandbox: Path of the file in the sandbox
path_of_the_file_to_download: Path of the file to download
path_of_the_uploaded_file_upload_file_op: Path of the uploaded file (upload file operation)
path_to_clone_the_repository_into: Path to clone the repository into
region_for_the_sandbox_e_g_us_eu: Region for the sandbox (e.g., us, eu)
run_artifacts_such_as_charts_run_code_op: Run artifacts such as charts (run code operation)
run_code_and_commands_in_secure_cloud_sa: Run code and commands in secure cloud sandboxes
run_python_javascript_or_typescript_in_a: Run Python, JavaScript, or TypeScript in an isolated Daytona sandbox and return the output. Use as a safe code interpreter for computation, parsing, or data wrangling.
sandbox_details_create_get_start_stop_de: Sandbox details (create, get, start, stop, delete operations)
sandbox_id_get_start_stop_and_delete_ope: Sandbox ID (get, start, stop, and delete operations also accept the sandbox name)
snapshot_id_or_name_uses_default_if_empt: Snapshot ID or name (uses default if empty)
snapshot_to_create_the_sandbox_from: Snapshot to create the sandbox from
upload_a_data_file_into_a_daytona_sandbo: Upload a data file into a Daytona sandbox, analyze it with Python, and download any generated results. Use for CSV/JSON analysis that needs real computation.
upload_file_to_send_to_the_sandbox: Upload file to send to the sandbox
whether_the_http_preview_is_public: Whether the HTTP preview is public
